prose: **Fukui Reactivity Analysis of Pyridine** — DFT, tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fitted) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry from fixed input file (not re-optimized). Neutral and charged (N+1 cation, N−1 anion) states calculated at multiplicity 2 via DFT SCF. Fukui indices computed via Mulliken population analysis: f⁺ (nucleophile attack susceptibility), f⁻ (electrophile attack susceptibility), f⁰ = (f⁺ + f⁻)/2 (biophilic index), and dual descriptor (f⁺ − f⁻). All calculations converged; charge conservation checks passed. **Most electrophilic site:** Nitrogen (atom 1), f⁺ = 0.4842 (dominant Fukui function for electrophilic chemistry). **Most nucleophilic sites:** Meta-carbon atoms (C3, C4), f⁻ = 0.1183. Pyridine exhibits strong electrophilic character at nitrogen (f⁰ = 0.4201), consistent with its π-basic nitrogen lone pair and expected to dominate electrophilic aromatic chemistry. Reactivity map: `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/pyridine/argo_claude-haiku-4.5/20260703-201218_pyridine_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png`. Command: `fukui --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --multiplicity 1 /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz`.
